skill.name: note-cleanup
skill.description: Clean messy meeting notes into structured markdown summaries. Use when asked to organize meeting notes, extract action items, separate decisions from open questions, or turn rough notes into a clean recap.
skill.summary: Transform rough meeting notes into structured markdown summaries with decisions, action items, and open questions.
skill.author: yaojingang

security_audit.summary: The skill contains only a SKILL.md instruction file and an agents/interface.yaml configuration file. Static analysis flagged two 'weak cryptographic algorithm' patterns, but these are false positives triggered by the words 'markdown' and 'recaps' in the natural-language descriptions. No code is executed; the skill only provides guidance for restructuring meeting notes. No scripts, network calls, filesystem operations, environment access, or external commands are present.

low_findings.title: False positive: weak cryptographic algorithm pattern
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at SKILL.md:3 and agents/interface.yaml:3. Both lines are plain English descriptions containing the words 'markdown' and 'recaps' — not cryptographic code. No hashing, encryption, or ciphers exist in this skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Verified both files contain only natural-language text; the pattern matches are triggered by substrings in descriptive sentences, not cryptographic operations.

content.user_title: Clean messy meeting notes into clear summaries
content.value_statement: Raw meeting notes are hard to read and easy to miscommunicate. This skill turns rough notes into structured markdown summaries with clear decisions, action items, and open questions.
actual_capabilities: Read raw meeting notes and identify key topics discussed
Extract decisions made during the meeting
List action items with owners when named in the notes
Separate open questions from resolved topics
Produce a clean markdown summary with consistent sections
Preserve important names, dates, and owners from the original notes
limitations: Cannot attend or join meetings — only processes notes you provide
Cannot infer owners or deadlines that were not mentioned in the notes
Does not transcribe audio or video recordings
Does not integrate with calendar or project management tools

use_cases.title: Team standup recap
use_cases.description: Convert quick standup notes into a shareable summary with blockers, decisions, and next steps for stakeholders who missed the meeting.

use_cases.title: Client meeting debrief
use_cases.description: Turn scribbled client call notes into a professional recap highlighting decisions, commitments, and follow-up questions for internal distribution.

use_cases.title: Personal note organization
use_cases.description: Restructure scattered personal meeting notes into a tidy markdown document for personal reference and future search.

prompt_templates.title: Basic note cleanup
prompt_templates.prompt: Clean these meeting notes into a markdown summary with sections for summary, decisions, action items, and open questions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick cleanup of standard meeting notes

prompt_templates.title: Focus on action items
prompt_templates.prompt: Read these notes and extract only the action items and their owners in a clean markdown list. Ignore everything else.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a quick task list from a meeting

prompt_templates.title: Decisions-only summary
prompt_templates.prompt: From these meeting notes, produce a short summary containing only the decisions that were made. Include who made each decision if mentioned.
prompt_templates.scenario: Producing a leadership update of what was decided

prompt_templates.title: Full structured recap with highlights
prompt_templates.prompt: Clean these meeting notes into a structured recap. Include: a one-paragraph summary, key decisions with context, action items with owners and dates, open questions, and a short highlights section for the most important points.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive recap for distribution to executives or stakeholders

output_examples.input: Notes: talked about Q3 launch. Mike said marketing ready by Sept 15. Priya worried about QA bandwidth. decided to push QA review to Sept 20. action: mike to send draft email by friday. question: do we need legal review on new terms?
output: Team aligned on Q3 launch timeline and identified QA bandwidth as a risk.
- Mike: send draft email by Friday.
- Do we need legal review on the new terms?

output_examples.input: Notes: standup 2026-06-19. sarah fixed login bug. tom blocked on api key. decided to use staging env for testing. action: tom to request new api key from ops.
output: Daily standup covered a completed login fix and a blocker on API access.
- Use the staging environment for testing.
- Tom: request a new API key from Ops.

best_practices: Provide the full notes in a single message so the skill has complete context before summarizing.
Include names and dates in the notes when they matter; the skill preserves them in the output.
Specify which sections you need when you want a shorter or focused output.
Review the generated summary and correct any inferred owners or deadlines before sharing.
anti_patterns: Do not paste partial notes and expect the skill to guess missing context.
Do not use this skill to summarize legal or contractual text without human review.
Do not rely on the output for assigning blame or recording sensitive personal information without verification.

faq.question: Does this skill access the internet or my files?
faq.answer: No. The skill only processes the text you provide in your message. It does not make network calls or read files on its own.

faq.question: Can it transcribe a recording?
faq.answer: No. You need to provide written notes. The skill works on text input only.

faq.question: What format is the output?
faq.answer: The output is markdown with sections for summary, decisions, action items, and open questions.

faq.question: Will it assign owners to action items automatically?
faq.answer: Only if a name is mentioned in the notes. It will not invent owners.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can I customize the output sections?
faq.answer: Yes. Ask for a specific structure in your prompt, such as decisions only or action items only.
